summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A square tile has a side length of 8 inches. A worker cuts the tile diagonally. What is the length of the diagonal cut?

A square cut diagonally forms a 45-45-90 triangle. Diagonal = side × √2 = 8√2 in.

An architect designs a triangular window shaped as a 30-60-90 triangle. If the shortest side is 2 feet, what is the area of the window?

Short leg = 2, long leg = 2√3. Area = ½(2)(2√3) = 2√3 ft².

A tent pole is the height of a 30-60-90 triangular cross-section. If the base of the tent (hypotenuse side on the ground) is 4 m, what is the pole height?

Hypotenuse = 4 → short leg = 2, long leg (height) = 2√3 m.

A solar panel is tilted at a 60° angle from the horizontal. If the panel is 2 m long, what is its vertical height above the mounting point?

Vertical height = 2 × sin 60° = 2 × (√3/2) = √3 m.
